title: TopBuild Acquires Specialty Products and Insulation (SPI) for $1 Billion
text:
TopBuild Corp. Strategic Acquisition of Specialty Products and Insulation (SPI):

Deal Overview:
TopBuild successfully acquired Specialty Products and Insulation (SPI) for $1.0 billion in cash, closing on October 7, 2025. SPI generated approximately $700 million in revenue and $75 million in EBITDA for the trailing twelve months ended June 30, 2025.

Expected Synergies:
TopBuild expects to realize approximately $35-$40 million in annual run-rate cost synergies within two years. Considering synergies of $35-$40 million within two years, the transaction multiple is 8.3x EBITDA, representing an attractive entry valuation.

Strategic Benefits:
The acquisition extends geographic footprint and expands mechanical insulation fabrication capabilities in a highly fragmented industry, enabling opportunities to drive operational efficiencies throughout the Specialty Distribution segment.

Recurring Revenue:
Approximately 55% of SPI's revenue is driven by recurring maintenance and repair, improving TopBuild's exposure to non-cyclical revenue streams and providing downside protection during housing cycles.

M&A Track Record:
M&A is a core strength of TopBuild, which has successfully completed 45 acquisitions since the spin-off in 2015 and generated an 18.2% return on invested capital as of December 31, 2024.
